contractSurface.securityClassification: tenant-scoped form authoring, submission validation, workflow dispatch, and analytics controls
coreGuarantees: Form schema publishing uses optimistic concurrency and preserves immutable published versions.
Publishing is blocked when fields or validation rules are missing.
Submission processing checks tenant ownership before validation and storage.
Submissions are validated only against the published form schema.
Validation errors are returned as business results rather than thrown exceptions.
Submissions are stored before downstream events are emitted.
Automation rule execution uses one SETNX lock per submission and rule.
Automation rule conditions select only the intended branch.
Analytics records are append-only and exclude PII fields.
Analytics aggregation remains tenant scoped and date partitioned.
Tenant context resolution remains automatic through TenantContextResolver.

packageSigningConstraint.reason: FLOW-21 can collect respondent PII and dispatch tenant workflows, so Tier 3 requires package-signing first pass.

machineLockedRules: OCC expectedVersion must match before publish
schema fields and validation rules must exist before publish
submitter tenant must match form tenant
validation uses the published schema only
submission storage happens before event emission
SETNX rule lock acquired before execution
one rule execution per submission and rule
rule tenant must match submission tenant
email, phone, ssn, password, and creditCard are excluded from analytics
analytics partitions include tenant and date window

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-21 operating values. These values may vary by tenant without changing schema immutability, ownership checks, published-schema validation, store-before-emit ordering, SETNX rule idempotency, PII exclusion, or tenant isolation.

keys.semantic: Maximum time allowed for schema fetch and publish operation.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune authoring responsiveness while OCC and immutability stay fixed.

keys.semantic: Whether publish is blocked when field validation rules are empty.

boundedRange.rationale: FLOW-21 certification keeps validation rules mandatory for published forms.

keys.semantic: Maximum time allowed to load schema and validate one submission.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune latency tolerance while ownership and schema validation remain mandatory.

keys.semantic: Maximum submitted fields accepted for one form submission.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may support larger forms while preventing runaway payloads.

keys.semantic: Time-to-live for one automation rule execution lock.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune workflow dedupe tolerance while SETNX idempotency stays fixed.

keys.semantic: Maximum time to evaluate workflow rule conditions for one submission.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune rule evaluation posture while conditional branch selection remains deterministic.

keys.semantic: Field names excluded from analytics records.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may add exclusions but cannot remove the certified PII baseline.

keys.semantic: Default window for aggregate submission metrics.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune reporting granularity while tenant/date partitioning remains fixed.
Tenants may tune reporting history while PII exclusion and append-only behavior stay fixed.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to form authoring responsiveness, submission size/latency posture, workflow rule timing, analytics retention, and customer-facing module naming.

edgeCases.name: Large intake forms
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A supports longer service-application forms with higher field limits.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T630 still validates against the published schema and stores before emitting events.

edgeCases.name: Strict published schema
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A keeps validation rules mandatory and shortens publish latency.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T629 still uses OCC and preserves immutable published versions.

edgeCases.name: Fast workflow routing
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A tunes rule locks and condition timeout for quick routing workflows.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T631 still uses SETNX per submission/rule and deterministic branch selection.

edgeCases.name: Privacy-forward analytics
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A adds more PII-style field names to the analytics exclusion list and keeps a shorter analytics retention window.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T632 still excludes the certified PII baseline, writes append-only records, and scopes metrics by tenant.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T629 through T632 remain fixed.
Factory IDs F1569 through F1576 remain fixed.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
Submissions validate against published schema only.
Submitter tenant must match form tenant.
Submission records are stored before events are emitted.
Rule execution remains SETNX locked per submission and rule.
Rule branch selection remains condition based.
Certified PII fields remain excluded from analytics.
Analytics metrics remain tenant scoped and date partitioned.
Tenant context resolution remains automatic through TenantContextResolver.

tenantACommunityIntake.description: Tenant A tunes Dynamic Forms and Workflows for community intake and responsive workflow routing.
tenantACommunityIntake.impact: Tenant A gets larger community intake forms, safer analytics, and tuned workflow routing while schema immutability, ownership checks, event ordering, rule idempotency, and tenant isolation stay unchanged.
tenantACommunityIntake.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-21 configuration values or display/module metadata.
All four services remain MicroserviceBase subclasses and use TenantContextResolver for tenant scope.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
